title: trigonochilum murinum
intro: Trigonochilum murinum, also known as the mouse's bane or Australian bloodroot, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its toxic alkaloids. Trigonochilum murinum has been used in herbal preparations to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and fever, and is also said to have anti-parasitic properties. However, due to its toxicity, the plant should be used with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional or experienced herbalist in herbal preparations such as tinctures, ointments, and infusions.
